english: bu-pa (bu pa), γcèr-nyal-mk'an (gcer nyal mkhan) Mil. (Ssk. nirgran-tha a naked man, gymnosophist; c'ós-sku (chos sku) γcèr (gcer) mtóṅ-du (mthong du) gról-bas (grol bas) having been deliver-ed so far as to see the c'ós-sku (chos sku) (v. sku (sku)) unveiled Glr.

(Lh. *šé-pa-*) dear, belov-ed, ... ltar (ltar) γcès-na (gces na) yaṅ (yang) although he is to me as dear as ... Glr.; ṅéd-kyi (nged kyi) mi (mi) γcès-pa (gces pa) a man dear to us, our beloved, our darling Mil.; γcès-ma (gces ma) a favourite, sweet-heart Cs.; γcès-prug (gces phrug) dear child Mil.; ex-cellent, precious, valuable, śin-tu (shin tu) γcès-pai (gces pai) lṅa (lnga) the five important letters (viz. the pre-fixed letters) Glr.; śès-pa (shes pa) γcès (gces) it is of im-portance to know Med.; often as super-lative: j̥ig ('jig) rtén (rten) d̥i-na ('di na) γcès-pa (gces pa) raṅ-srog (rang srog) yin (yin) the dearest thing in the world is one's own life Pth.; γcès-par (gces par) byèd-pa (byed pa) Stg., d̥zin-pa ('dzin pa) Glr. c. accus, W.: *šé-pa cò-ce* gen. with the dat., to hold dear, to love, to esteem, persons or things, but not appli-cable to the deeper affections of the heart. -- γcès-bsdús (gces bsdus) Lex. w.e.; γcès-btus (gces btus) Cs. choice pieces (out of books).

pf. bcag (bcag), imp. cog(s) (chog(s)), W. *càg-cè*, imp. *cog* trs. to càg-pa (chag pa), to break, dúm-bur (dum bur) to pieces; to break off, or asunder; to smash, a glass; to crack, nuts; to burst, split, blast, a gun, a rock; fig.: to break, to violate, a promise, a vow, a law etc. frq., yáb-kyi (yab kyi) bka (bka') bcàg-tu (bcag tu) med (med) the word of my father may not be violated (by me) Glr.

consumption, phthisis, γcoṅ - cén (gcong chen) dmú - c'u (dmu chu) prob. dropsy in the chest or in the pericardium Med.; gen. any chronic disease *coṅ-la tsu' ma soṅ-ṅam* C. it has not taken a chronic turn, has it? also fig.: *sem coṅ-po dug* C. the heart is sick, af-flicted.
Lex., Sch.: lamentations, wailings, plaintive voices, cf. γcoṅ-ba (gcong ba).

1. pf. bsoṅs (bshongs), to excavate, wash out, undermine through the action of water, túr-du (thur du) γcoṅ-bar (gcong bar) mi (mi) gyúr-ro (gyur ro) they are not undermined (by the water) Stg.; γcoṅ-roṅ (gcong rong) a narrow passage, a defilé Cs. -- 2. from γcoṅ (gcong), to get faint, languid, wearied in mind, C.

pf. bcad (bcad), fut. γcad (gcad), imp. cod (cod), W. *càd-cè*, imp. *cod* 1. to cut, c'ád-bya (chad bya) γcód-pa (gcod pa) secanda secare Gram.; to cut asunder, k'am-tsad-du (kham tshad du) into small bits; to cut off, chop off, the hands; to cut down, to fell, trees; to cut out, the tongue Dzl.; to rend asunder, to break, a thread, a rope, chains, fetters. -- 2. to cut off fig.: c'u (chu), the water, by damming it out, frq.; to reduce, the wages; to cure, a disease; to suppress, a passion; to discontinue, to give up, zan (zan), zas (zas), eating i.e. to abstain from food, to fast; srog (srog), to kill, to murder, frq.; to stop a thing in its origin, to obviate, prevent, avert; to avoid; to lock, the door, frq.; ... kyi (kyi), or la (la), bár-du (bar du) γcód-pa (gcod pa) to throw obstacles in a person's way, to hinder, impede, frq.; sróg-la (srog la) bár-du (bar du) γcód-pa (gcod pa) dé-dag (de dag) all these life-endangering beings Glr.; (for more examples refer to bar (bar)); to stop, to make a pause, in reading, śad (shad) yoṅ-na (yong na) drág-por (drag por) bcàd-pa (bcad pa) making a marked stop, when there is a shad, Gram.; rnam(-par) (rnam (par)) γcód(-pa) (gcod (pa)), or bcad(-pa) (bcad (pa)), section, paragraph; stop, pause; yoṅs-γcód (yongs gcod) id. Gram.; to de-cide, cès (ces) bcàd-do (bcad do) thus he decided Dzl.; k'rims (khrims); or (Dzl.) źal-c'é (zhal che), to pass sentence or judgment; to judge, condemn, cf. also tág-γcód-pa (thag gcod pa). -- 3. to cross (little used), c'u-bo (chu bo) grú-yis (gru yis) a river in a boat Glr. -- 4. rjes (rjes) γcód-pa (gcod pa) to follow the track, used both of men and dogs; *már - dzi* (to follow) the smell of butter (viz. of roast-meat), *kyúr-dzi cọ̀-pa* C. to follow the sourish smell (viz. the smell of beer); (γ)sár- ((g)sar) (also tsar (tshar) Pth.) γcód-pa (gcod pa) to search into, to in-vestigate, to examine or study thoroughly Ld.-Glr. Schl. p. 20, b. -- c'ád-pas (chad pas) γcód-pa (gcod pa) and other phrases v. under the re-spective noun. -- *cọ̀-táṅ* C. the Tibetan rupee, having lines (radii) of division mark-ed, by which they may be cut into smal-ler pieces. -- Note: In some phrases the
